title: Add team lead role with create-project permission
description: Assign team lead (leader_id) to teams table based on employee who leads most projects per team. Team leads get create-project permission. Dashboard badges change from Ketua Proyek to Ketua Tim for team leads.

title: Phase 4: WorkItemDetail.vue + WorkItemDetailController + timeline + resubmit
description:
## Why
MK-ys5 Phase 4. The leaf page — intentionally the heavy one, but bounded to one work item. This is where the actual report entry and approval timeline lives.

## What to do
1. WorkItemDetailController::show(WorkItem $workItem)
   - Authorize: employee must be assigned to this work item OR be the project leader
   - Employee props: work item, their assignment target, all their reports for this year with reviews, year
   - Lead props: work item, all assignments (employee + their reports + reviews), year
   - Reports include: performanceReportReviews (ordered by created_at), attachments
2. ReportResubmitController::store(PerformanceReport $report)
   - Authorize: employee must be the reporter AND status must be 'rejected'
   - Validate: realization (numeric), issues/solutions/action_plan (nullable string)
   - Update report: new realization/notes, status='pending', clear reviewed_by/review_note
   - Insert PerformanceReportReview(action='resubmitted', actor=auth user)
   - Return back()->with('success', ...)
3. Pages/Performance/WorkItemDetail.vue (role-aware)
   - Employee side:
     - Item description + personal assignment target
     - Month list: for each month that has a report OR is editable, show status chip + realization
     - Active month entry form: realization input, kendala/solusi/RTL, submit button
     - Rejected months show 'Ajukan Ulang' button that shows edit form
     - Approved months are read-only
     - Attachment panel (only when report exists)
     - PerformanceTimeline below entry form
   - Lead side:
     - Item description + all assigned members listed
     - Per member: all their reports as MemberReportCard
     - Each card: month label, realization, kendala/solusi/RTL, attachments, timeline, approve/reject form
4. Components/Performance/PerformanceTimeline.vue
   - Props: reviews: Array<{ actor_name, action, note, created_at }>
   - Vertical stepper with icon per action type (submitted=blue, resubmitted=purple, approved=green, rejected=red)
   - Timestamp shown as relative (2 hari lalu) with tooltip for exact date
5. Routes: GET /performance/work-items/{workItem}, PATCH /performance/{report}/resubmit

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Employee can view and edit reports per month
- [ ] Approved months are read-only
- [ ] Rejected months show Ajukan Ulang button
- [ ] Resubmit resets status to pending + creates resubmitted review entry
- [ ] Timeline shows full sequence of events in correct order
- [ ] Lead sees all members' reports with their timelines
- [ ] Lead can approve/reject with note from this page
- [ ] Attachment upload works from this page
close_reason: Phase 4 complete: WorkItemDetail, PerformanceTimeline, ReportResubmitController all implemented and tested (10 tests, 79 assertions). Committed 2dc914d.

title: Phase 3: ProjectDetail.vue + ProjectDetailController
description:
## Why
MK-ys5 Phase 3. The second level of the navigation hierarchy — shows work items for one project with aggregated status, no full report bodies loaded.

## What to do
1. ProjectDetailController::show(Project $project)
   - Authorize: employee must be a member OR the project leader
   - Employee view props: work items with their assignment target for this employee, per-item approval status summary (any pending, any rejected, all approved), year
   - Lead view props: work items with member breakdown (count per status: pending/approved/rejected per member), year
   - No attachment data. No full report bodies. No kendala/solusi/RTL text at this level.
2. Pages/Performance/ProjectDetail.vue
   - Header: project name, team, year
   - Employee: table rows — item #, description, personal target, pct, status chip. 'Laporan' link → /performance/work-items/{id}
   - Lead: table rows — item #, description, members count, pending badge, 'Tinjau' link → /performance/work-items/{id}?role=lead
   - Expandable row for lead: shows member names with per-member pending/approved/rejected pill counts (no report text)
3. Add route: GET /performance/projects/{project} → ProjectDetailController@show → performance.projects.show

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Employee can reach this page from ProjectList card
- [ ] Employee sees only their assigned work items with their personal targets
- [ ] Lead sees all work items with member status counts
- [ ] No performance_report.realization or issues/solutions/action_plan in page props
- [ ] Link to WorkItemDetail works (can 404 until Phase 4)
close_reason: ProjectDetailController + ProjectDetail.vue live; 124/124 tests; committed fca498d

title: Phase 2: ProjectList.vue + ProjectListController
description:
## Why
MK-ys5 Phase 2. Replace the heavyweight /performance page with a lightweight project-card list. Depends on Phase 1 (MK-ys5-p1) existing but not on its data being used in UI yet.

## What to do
1. ProjectListController::index
   - Employee side: Project withCount(assigned items, items with ≥1 approved report, pending approvals)
   - Lead side: Project withCount(work_items, pending_reviews, members) where leader_id=employee
   - No eager loading of work_items or performance_reports — counts only
   - Props: { employee_projects: [...], lead_projects: [...], year: int }
2. Pages/Performance/ProjectList.vue
   - Employee tab: project cards grouped by team, shows assigned/done counts, link to /performance/projects/{id}
   - Lead tab: project cards with pending review badge, link to same route
   - Year selector (replaces year+month selector — month no longer needed at this level)
3. Update route: GET /performance → ProjectListController@index (keep old route as /performance/legacy for safety)
4. Update all nav links that point to performance.index

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] /performance loads without work_items or performance_reports in page props
- [ ] Employee cards show correct assigned item count
- [ ] Lead cards show correct pending review count  
- [ ] Year selector changes the counts
- [ ] Clicking a card navigates to /performance/projects/{id} (can 404 until Phase 3)
close_reason: ProjectListController + ProjectList.vue live; 116/116 tests; committed 62dfaa6

title: Phase 1: performance_report_reviews DB + wire into actions
description:
## Why
MK-ys5 Phase 1. Add the append-only audit table and wire it into existing save/approve/reject flows. No UI changes — this is purely additive and backward compatible.

## What to do
1. Migration: create_performance_report_reviews_table
   - id, performance_report_id (FK cascade), actor_id (FK SET NULL → users), action ENUM(submitted,resubmitted,approved,rejected), note TEXT, created_at
2. PerformanceReportReview model (hasMany from PerformanceReport)
3. Modify SavePerformanceBatchAction: after upsert, if new report → insert submitted review; if existing and was rejected → insert resubmitted review
4. Modify PerformanceApprovalController::approve → insert approved review
5. Modify PerformanceApprovalController::reject → insert rejected review with note
6. Tests: ReportReviewTest covering all four action types

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Migration runs clean
- [ ] Saving a new report creates a submitted review record
- [ ] Saving a previously-rejected report creates a resubmitted review record
- [ ] Approving creates an approved review record
- [ ] Rejecting with note creates a rejected review record with note
- [ ] Tests pass
close_reason: migration + model + review records wired into save/approve/reject actions; 112/112 tests pass; committed 89e9a0e

title: Lead review: dedicated project review page with approval timeline
description:
## Why this issue exists

Performance/Index.vue is 1854 lines serving both employee entry and lead review. The backend loads every project → every work item → every report for the year → every attachment in a single Inertia page prop. At scale (10 projects × 15 items × 12 months × 5 attachments) this causes DOM memory explosion and full-page reloads on every action. There is also no submission history — approve/reject has no audit trail, and rejected reports cannot be resubmitted with context.

The user wants: project list page → project detail → per-work-item detail with timeline. Both employee and lead sides need this three-level hierarchy.

## Architecture: three-level navigation

Both roles share the same URL structure; controller/page is role-aware.

    /performance                        → lightweight project cards (counts only)
    /performance/projects/{project}     → work item table + status summary
    /performance/work-items/{workItem}  → entry form + approval timeline (role-aware)

### Level 1 — /performance (replaces Index.vue)
Backend: withCount subqueries only — no nested eager loading of work_items or performance_reports.
Employee card: project name, team, assigned item count, pending approval count.
Lead card: project name, team, pending review badge.

### Level 2 — /performance/projects/{project}
Backend: work items + aggregated report status counts per member. No full report data. No attachments.
Employee: table of their assigned items with pct + status summary. Link to WorkItemDetail.
Lead: table of all items with member breakdown (pending/approved/rejected per item). Expandable rows for quick review.

### Level 3 — /performance/work-items/{workItem}  (the intentionally heavy page, bounded to one item)
Employee: assignment target, edit form per month (locked for approved), attachment upload, timeline.
Lead: all assigned members, per-member all-month reports with full review timeline, approve/reject with comment.

## Database: performance_report_reviews (append-only)

    id
    performance_report_id  FK → performance_reports (cascade delete)
    actor_id               FK → users (SET NULL on delete)
    action                 ENUM: submitted | resubmitted | approved | rejected
    note                   TEXT nullable
    created_at             TIMESTAMP (no updated_at — write-once)

Full history preserved through reject → resubmit → approve cycles.

## Resubmit flow

    Employee saves       → report row (status=pending) + review(submitted)
    Lead rejects         → status=rejected, review_note set + review(rejected, note)
    Employee resubmits   → PATCH /performance/{report}/resubmit → status=pending + review(resubmitted)
    Lead approves        → status=approved + review(approved)

## Files

New pages:
  Pages/Performance/ProjectList.vue       (replaces Index.vue)
  Pages/Performance/ProjectDetail.vue
  Pages/Performance/WorkItemDetail.vue    (role-aware: employee form vs lead review)

New components:
  Components/Performance/PerformanceTimeline.vue   (vertical stepper of review events)
  Components/Performance/AttachmentPanel.vue       (extracted from Index.vue)
  Components/Performance/WorkItemReportForm.vue    (employee entry)
  Components/Performance/MemberReportCard.vue      (lead per-member card)

New backend:
  ProjectDetailController
  WorkItemDetailController
  PerformanceReportReview model + migration
  ReportResubmitController

Modified:
  SavePerformanceBatchAction  → insert submitted/resubmitted review record
  PerformanceApprovalController → insert approved/rejected review record
  PerformanceController::index  → simplify to lightweight withCount query
  routes/web.php               → add 3 new GET routes + PATCH resubmit

## Phased delivery

Phase 1 — DB + model + wire review records into existing actions (no UI, low risk)
Phase 2 — ProjectList.vue + ProjectListController (replaces Index.vue)
Phase 3 — ProjectDetail.vue + ProjectDetailController
Phase 4 — WorkItemDetail.vue + WorkItemDetailController + PerformanceTimeline.vue + resubmit
Phase 5 — Cleanup (delete Index.vue, remove legacy batch, fire lead notification)

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] /performance loads in <500ms with 20+ projects (only counts loaded)
- [ ] Project cards link to /performance/projects/{id}
- [ ] ProjectDetail shows work item status table without loading full report bodies
- [ ] WorkItemDetail shows entry form + timeline for employee
- [ ] WorkItemDetail shows member reports + timeline for lead
- [ ] Timeline shows submitted → rejected → resubmitted → approved sequence
- [ ] Lead can approve/reject with comment from timeline
- [ ] Employee can resubmit rejected report
- [ ] Resubmit creates new review record, resets status to pending
- [ ] No N+1 queries on any of the three levels
close_reason: All 5 phases complete and merged. DB audit log (Phase 1), ProjectList (Phase 2), ProjectDetail (Phase 3), WorkItemDetail + timeline + resubmit (Phase 4), cleanup (Phase 5). 134 tests passing.

title: Bug: Laporan Pegawai shows 100% for all employees even when no tasks assigned
description: In Laporan Pegawai, all employees show 100% achievement even though only 'Sukma' was assigned and completed tasks. The report query is likely computing avg_achievement across the wrong scope - employees with no reports should show 0% or N/A, not 100%. Root cause: likely a JOIN that returns rows for all employees and AVG(NULL) or AVG(0) computes wrong percentage.
close_reason: Added whereColumn('reported_by', employee_id) to both top10 and achievementSub queries in EmployeeReportController — was crediting every project member for every report submitted by anyone in the same project

title: Bug: dashboard Proyek Saya shows NaN% for project progress
description: NaN% appears in the Proyek Saya dashboard section for ALL users (staff, head, lead), not just lead. 'Pengembangan Inovasi' project shows NaN% with red progress bar and '2 item kerja'. Root cause: achievement_percentage is cast as decimal:2 in Laravel which serializes to a STRING in JSON. The JS reduce accumulator loses numeric type when adding a string, causing NaN. Fix: coerce to Number in projectAvg function in Dashboard.vue.
close_reason: Fixed projectAvg in Dashboard.vue:185 — coerce achievement_percentage to Number since Laravel decimal:2 cast serializes as string in JSON

title: Bug: lead cannot review kendala/solution/tindak-lanjut from employee side
description: In the Tim Saya (team lead) view, the lead cannot see or review kendala, solusi, and rencana tindak lanjut submitted by employees. Whatever is shown in the lead side should be visible for review.
close_reason: Added issues/solutions/action_plan to TeamWorkItemReport interface and display in lead Tim Saya view under each member's current-month report

title: Bug: lead view shows current month only, should show cumulative progress
description: In the Tim Saya view, the lead sees only the current month's realization. It should show cumulative progress across all months (data from previous months should also appear), with the current month's contribution highlighted.
close_reason: getMemberReports returns all months sorted newest-first; committed 77f195c

title: Bug: work item edit (rincian kegiatan) save does nothing
description: When a project leader tries to edit a work item (rincian kegiatan) in Kinerja Saya tab - e.g. changing target_unit from 'Kegiatan' to 'Website' - clicking Simpan does nothing. No request is sent or the response is silently failing. Validation errors are also invisible in the Tim Saya edit form (no InputError components).
close_reason: Fixed: numeric validation (was integer), WorkItemUpdatedNotification dispatched on update, InputError added to lead edit form, step/min updated to allow decimals

title: Bug: batch performance save returns 500 on success
description: The batch performance save endpoint (performance.batch) returns HTTP 500 even when data is successfully stored. This causes the UI to show an error even though the save worked.
close_reason: notifications table was bundled into the approval migration causing duplicate table error. Extracted into dedicated artisan-generated migration (2026_04_12_135504). Removed from approval migration down() as well.

title: Bug: batch performance save updates all entries when only one changed
description: When an employee inputs performance data and saves, ALL entries are updated even if only one entry was modified. There is also no way to save individual entries - only a single batch save button exists. The batch save returns a 500 error even though data is stored.
close_reason: Replaced shared batch form with per-item reactive state; each work item now has its own Simpan button; removed sticky batch save; items are seeded lazily on first interaction

title: Timeline: show HH:mm time alongside date
description: In PerformanceTimeline.vue, formatDate() currently only shows date (day/month/year). It needs to also show the time (HH:mm) so reviewers can see exactly when events happened.
close_reason: Completed in feature/employee-profile-mutation

title: Timeline: show speech bubble for rejection/note comments
description: In PerformanceTimeline.vue the rv.note field (e.g. rejection reason) is currently rendered as plain italic text. It should appear as a styled speech bubble that visually connects to its timeline event. Design: colored background box with a small triangle pointer, red tint for rejection, gray for others.
close_reason: Completed in feature/employee-profile-mutation

title: Replace native confirm/alert with shadcn AlertDialog
description: All destructive actions currently use native browser confirm(). Replace with shadcn AlertDialog for consistent UX. Affected: WorkItemDetail.vue deleteReport(), Notifications/Index.vue deleteAll(), AppLayout.vue deleteNotification() inline X button. Create a reusable ConfirmDialog composable or component.
close_reason: Completed in feature/employee-profile-mutation

title: Phase 5: cleanup — delete Index.vue, remove legacy batch, lead notification
description:
## Why
MK-ys5 Phase 5. Final cleanup after all new pages are live and confirmed working. Remove the old monolith and legacy route.

## What to do
1. Delete Pages/Performance/Index.vue
2. Remove PerformanceController::index personalProjects/teamProjects/inlineAssignmentTarget helpers
3. Change GET /performance to point to ProjectListController (remove /performance/legacy fallback)
4. Optionally remove POST /performance/batch route if all entry is now via WorkItemDetail
5. Fire lead notification when employee submits a report (MK backlog item): 
   - In SavePerformanceBatchAction, after insert, notify project leader if first submission for this month
   - Reuse or create ReportSubmittedNotification

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Index.vue deleted
- [ ] No orphan imports referencing Index.vue
- [ ] /performance redirects correctly
- [ ] Lead receives in-app notification when team member submits a report
close_reason: Phase 5 complete: Index.vue deleted, PerformanceController trimmed to storeBatch only. Lead notification already wired via PerformanceBatchSubmitted → NotifyTeamLeadOnReportSubmitted. All 134 tests pass. Committed dea992e.

title: Notification to lead not sent when team member submits report
description: Lead should receive a database notification when a team member submits their performance report. The NotifyTeamLeadOnReportSubmitted listener exists and is wired to PerformanceBatchSubmitted, but was broken by a PHP parse error (??  inside string interpolation) in ReportSubmittedNotification.php line 38. The parse error is now fixed. Verify the notification also contains a URL so lead can click through to review. Check: ReportSubmittedNotification should include 'url' => route('performance.index') in toArray().
close_reason: Root cause was PHP parse error (?? inside string interpolation) in ReportSubmittedNotification.php line 38 — fixed. Added url field to notification payload.

title: Feature: dispatch WorkItemAssignedNotification when task assigned
description: WorkItemAssignedNotification class exists but is never dispatched. Should be sent in SyncWorkItemAssignmentsAction when new assignments are created, so employees receive notifications when assigned tasks by their lead.
close_reason: Dispatch WorkItemAssignedNotification in WorkItemController::store after sync. Added url to notification data. Notification items in dropdown are now clickable — marks as read and navigates to performance.index.

title: Feature: individual performance entry save (per work item)
description: Employees need to be able to save each work item entry individually without saving all entries at once. Currently only a batch save button exists at the bottom of the form.
close_reason: superseded by MK-ipv per-item save — each item now has its own Simpan button

title: Verify and seed BPS Sulteng logo asset
description: AppLayout references BPS logo at public/images/bps-sulteng-logo.png but the file does not exist yet. Need to add the actual BPS Sulteng logo (or a placeholder SVG) at that path so the sidebar renders correctly.
close_reason: Closed

title: Add work items to seeder data
description: seeder_data.json currently has no work_items key so WorkItemSeeder skips silently. Need to extract work items from example_static/matriks-kinerja-v9-with-data.html and add them under each project in seeder_data.json, then verify WorkItemSeeder creates them correctly.
close_reason: Work items live in IndexedDB in the prototype (not static). Generated 3 placeholder items per project (prep/execution/report) — 417 items across 139 projects. seeder_data.json now tracked in git.

title: Write Pest tests for models, actions, and policies
description: Phase-0 bootstrap is complete but has no test coverage. Need unit tests for Employee/Project/WorkItem/PerformanceReport models (relationships, scopes), feature tests for SavePerformanceBatchAction and SavePerformanceReportAction, and policy tests for PerformancePolicy (store/update scoping by project membership).
close_reason: 47 tests passing: Employee/Project unit tests, SavePerformanceReport/Batch action tests, PerformancePolicy tests for project membership scoping.

title: Refactor Dashboard.vue into smaller components
description: Dashboard.vue is 1600+ lines with 3 role dashboards (staff/head/admin), chart configs, and repeated template blocks. Extract into focused components: TeamBarChart, EmployeeProjectsChart, EmployeeAchievementChart, TrendLineChart, EmployeeRankingList, PersonalStatsCard, TeamRankingSection. Move chart data/accessors into composables (useDashboardCharts). Each role dashboard should be its own component composed from shared building blocks.

title: Feature: notification system table format
description: User wants the notification display to use a table format instead of the current dropdown list in AppLayout. This may involve a dedicated Notifications/Index page or redesigning the bell dropdown.
close_reason: Completed in feature/employee-profile-mutation

title: Write Vitest tests for composables and Pinia stores
description: Frontend composables (useFilters, usePerformanceForm) and Pinia stores (sidebar, toast) have no tests. Add Vitest unit tests covering: useFilters filter state and applyFilters navigation, usePerformanceForm item initialization and batch form logic, sidebar open/close/toggle, toast enqueue/dequeue/auto-remove.
close_reason: Closed

title: Update auth pages to use AppLayout/BPS branding
description: Login and Register pages still use Breeze's default GuestLayout. Need to swap to a BPS-branded GuestLayout (or AppLayout) with BPS Sulteng logo and #1B4B8A color scheme. Also check if profile page needs AppLayout swap from Breeze default.
close_reason: Closed
